summary: Russia has established at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarus border, enhancing its strike capabilities. Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV engagements across northern and central regions, targeting Russian positions and infrastructure. Air defense collaborations between Ukraine and Western partners are advancing, aiming to improve interception of Russian drones.
key_points: Russia created at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarus border, including a large complex near the village of Tsimbulova in Oryol region (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Ukrainian UAVs from Chernihiv region moved northward toward Kyiv region along the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/64997).
Ukrainian forces struck the "Vostochny" training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying Russian air defenses and personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Ukrainian UAVs operated from Sumy and Kharkiv regions targeting enemy positions (Source: @eRadarrua/96312).
A partnership was signed between Ukrainian SkyFall and Airbus Defence to integrate P1‑SUN interceptors, which have reportedly downed about 10,000 Russian drones (Source: @DeepStateUA/23574).
Ukrainian forces destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, located in civilian buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).
Russian forces claimed progress on all fronts and destruction of Ukrainian assets, including a Leopard‑2 tank (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Potential launch of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from occupied Crimea was reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67579).

sections.executive_summary: Russia has established at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarus border, expanding its UAV strike capacity. Ukrainian forces continue active UAV operations across northern, central, and eastern regions, targeting Russian positions and infrastructure. Ongoing defense cooperation, including a SkyFall‑Airbus partnership, aims to boost Ukraine's air defense against the increased Russian drone threat.

points: Ukrainian UAVs launched from Chernihiv region moved northward along the Belarus border toward Kyiv region, indicating reconnaissance and strike missions in the northern axis (Source: @kpszsu/64997).

Ukrainian forces hit the "Vostochny" training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying Russian TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 air defense systems, as well as personnel and missile calculations (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Ukrainian UAVs operated from Sumy and Kharkiv regions, with reported strikes on enemy positions, reflecting coordinated air‑ground operations (Source: @eRadarrua/96312).
In Pokrovsk, Ukrainian troops destroyed six Russian UAV launch points located in civilian structures such as a café and a children's goods store (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).

Ukrainian SkyFall and Airbus Defence signed a memorandum to integrate P1‑SUN interceptors, which have reportedly downed around 10,000 Russian drones, enhancing air defense capabilities (Source: @DeepStateUA/23574).

Russia established at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarus border, including a large complex near Tsimbulova in Oryol region, described as the "largest drone port in the world" (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).

Russian forces claim advancement on all fronts, stating they have destroyed Ukrainian assets, including a Leopard‑2 tank, and shot down 15 guided bombs and 540 UAVs in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Russian statements assert that Ukrainian attacks cannot halt their momentum and that they are moving forward across all directions of the special military operation (Source: @tass_agency/380486).
Reports indicate possible launch of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from occupied Crimea, highlighting a strategic missile threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67579).
Russian air defense units, such as TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23, were targeted and destroyed at the Vostochny training ground, indicating vulnerability to Ukrainian strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed operating in the Dnipropetrovsk direction, moving from Kryvyi Rih district toward Kamianets district, suggesting pressure on Russian-held areas (Source: @kpszsu/65004).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV group moving in the north‑east of Dnipropetrovsk region, heading west/north, indicating reconnaissance over contested zones (Source: @kpszsu/64992).
A UAV pair was noted over Synelnykove in Dnipropetrovsk region, highlighting continued aerial activity in the eastern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/96315).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported from the north of Kharkiv region, moving southward toward Kharkiv, showing pressure on Russian positions in the east (Source: @kpszsu/65003).
Ukrainian UAVs from Sumy region were directed toward Zhitomir, indicating cross‑regional strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/96360).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported in Chernihiv region moving northward along the Belarus border toward Kyiv, reflecting heightened activity on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/64997).
UAVs were observed on the north side of Chernihiv, moving southwest, indicating varied directional operations (Source: @kpszsu/64964).
A UAV was reported south of Slavutych in Chernihiv, heading south/southeast, showing penetration into central Ukraine from the north (Source: @kpszsu/65009).
UAV activity was noted along the Dnipro highway in Chernihiv, moving toward Dobryanka, suggesting targeting of logistical routes (Source: @eRadarrua/96349).
UAVs were seen on the north side of Chernihiv along the border near Slavutych, moving southward, indicating continued pressure along the border corridor (Source: @eRadarrua/96369).
Ukrainian forces reported strikes on bridges connecting Crimea to the mainland, indicating attempts to disrupt Russian logistics in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
A Ukrainian strike hit the Temryuk sea port on the Kuban, causing a fire and casualties, demonstrating reach into Russian-occupied southern territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from the south‑west toward Dnipro/Kamianets, suggesting operations targeting Russian positions in the south‑central axis (Source: @kpszsu/65004).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted in the Poltava region moving from Karlivka toward Poltava, indicating pressure on Russian‑held areas in the south‑west (Source: @eRadarrua/96350).
Ukrainian forces warned of a high probability of Russian "Oreshnyk" missile use from the Kapustin Yar training ground, highlighting a missile threat on the southern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81873).
Ukrainian SkyFall and Airbus partnership aims to integrate P1‑SUN interceptors, which have reportedly downed about 10,000 Russian drones, enhancing air defense (Source: @DeepStateUA/23574).
Russian forces claimed to have shot down 15 guided bombs and 540 UAVs in a single day, indicating intense aerial engagements (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple strikes on Russian air defense assets, including TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 systems at the Vostochny training ground (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Reports of Russian drone launch sites near the Belarus border suggest increased UAV sortie rates, complicating Ukrainian air defense (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Potential launch of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from occupied Crimea adds a strategic missile dimension to the air war (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67579).

key_developments: Russia has established at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarus border, including a large complex near Tsimbulova in Oryol region, expanding its UAV strike capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Ukrainian forces destroyed six Russian UAV launch points in Pokrovsk, located in civilian structures, reducing enemy aerial launch capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81893).

Ukrainian SkyFall and Airbus Defence signed a memorandum to integrate P1‑SUN interceptors, which have reportedly downed about 10,000 Russian drones, strengthening air defense (Source: @DeepStateUA/23574).
Ukrainian strikes hit the Vostochny training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying Russian TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 air defense systems and personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Russian forces claim to have destroyed a Ukrainian Leopard‑2 tank and shot down 540 UAVs in a single day, indicating high-intensity combat (Source: @tass_agency/380480).
Potential launch of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from occupied Crimea was reported, raising concerns of a new strategic missile threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67579).
Ukrainian UAVs operated from multiple northern regions, moving along the Belarus border toward Kyiv, showing sustained pressure on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/64997).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian expansion of drone launch sites near the Belarus border significantly enhances its ability to conduct massed UAV attacks, complicating Ukrainian air defense. The reported potential use of Iskander‑M missiles from Crimea adds a high‑precision, long‑range threat to critical infrastructure. Ukrainian counter‑UAV operations, including the destruction of launch points and integration of advanced interceptors, are mitigating but face escalating Russian aerial capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are deepening defense cooperation, exemplified by the SkyFall‑Airbus memorandum to field P1‑SUN interceptors, bolstering Ukraine's air defense network. Regional NATO allies are likely to increase support for air defense systems to counter the surge in Russian UAV activity. Ukraine's diplomatic outreach continues, seeking further assistance to sustain its defensive posture across all fronts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Satellite analysis confirms the new Russian drone sites are within tens of kilometers of the Belarusian border, indicating a strategic use of Belarusian airspace for launch corridors. UAV movement patterns from Chernihiv to Kyiv suggest coordinated reconnaissance and strike missions targeting logistics hubs. Continuous monitoring of missile launch capabilities from Crimea is essential due to the reported Iskander‑M activity.
